title: David Petraeus on Office Hours Podcast
summary: U.S. Army General (ret.) and former CIA Director David Petraeus speaks with Aroop Mukharji (@aroopmukharji) about ISIS, America’s other challenges in the world, how he gets his news, and his ideal vacation.
body: David H. Petraeus is a retired four-star Army general and former director of the Central Intelligence Agency. Currently, he is a non-resident senior fellow at Harvard Kennedy School’s Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs where he is working with Center Director Graham Allison to explore renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness. Their work focuses on major technological, scientific and economic dynamics that are spurring renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness, exploring in particular the impact of the ongoing revolutions in energy, information technology, advanced manufacturing, and life sciences. Petraeus spent 37 years in the Army, including roles as commander of U.S. and international military forces in Afghanistan, commander of U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), and commanding general of the multi-national force in Iraq. _list.source: Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School

result.main_content_en: The article profiles David H. Petraeus, a retired four-star general and former CIA director, in his current role as a non-resident senior fellow at Harvard Kennedy School's Belfer Center. He is collaborating with Center Director Graham Allison to analyze the drivers of renewed U.S. and North American competitiveness. Their research specifically examines the transformative impact of ongoing revolutions in key sectors: energy, information technology, advanced manufacturing, and life sciences. Petraeus's extensive 37-year military career, which included commanding multinational forces in Iraq and Afghanistan as well as leading U.S. Central Command, provides a foundational background for this work on strategic national competitiveness shaped by technological and economic dynamics.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了美国退役四星上将、前中央情报局局长戴维·H·彼得雷乌斯，他目前是哈佛大学肯尼迪学院贝尔弗科学与国际事务中心的非常驻高级研究员。他正与中心主任格雷厄姆·艾利森合作，共同探讨重振美国和北美竞争力的关键因素。他们的研究工作聚焦于推动竞争力复兴的重大技术、科学和经济动态，特别深入探讨了能源、信息技术、先进制造业和生命科学等领域持续发生的革命性变革所带来的影响。彼得雷乌斯拥有37年的军旅生涯，曾担任驻伊拉克和阿富汗多国部队指挥官、美国中央司令部司令等要职，这一丰富的战略背景为其从科技与经济动态角度研究国家竞争力提供了重要基础。
